description_long: This is a yellow Royal Air Force (RAF) rescue helicopter with a streamlined fuselage and a prominent cockpit at the front. The helicopter features a large main rotor with four long, dark blades and a smaller tail rotor at the rear. The fuselage is marked with 'RAF RESCUE' and roundel insignia, and it has a distinctive black nose and black striping along the sides. The cockpit windows are large, rectangular, and arranged in a wraparound configuration, providing wide visibility for the crew. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail wheel, with no visible skids. The surface appears metallic and smooth, with some matte and glossy elements.
description: A yellow RAF rescue helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, black striping, and large wraparound cockpit windows. Marked with 'RAF RESCUE' and roundel insignia, it features wheeled landing gear and a streamlined body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded cockpit, large wraparound windows, and the full span of the four main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the 'RAF RESCUE' markings, roundel insignia, and the full length of the fuselage and tail boom.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Right side view with similar markings as the left, showing the cockpit profile and the tail structure.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of composite or metal, colored dark gray with yellow tips, each blade approximately 7-8 meters in length.
attribute_tail_rotor: Present, with four short blades arranged in a cross pattern, mounted vertically on the left side of the tail; blades are dark with yellow tips.
attribute_landing_skids: Not present; the helicopter uses a tricycle-style wheeled landing gear with two main wheels and a tail wheel, all with visible struts and supports.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Five large, rectangular cockpit windows arranged in a wraparound configuration at the front, with clear glass and black framing.
